rulings.Case Title: U.S.BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE FOR VS. JAMES LANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JULY 31, 2007, LINE 3. CROSS-DEFENDANT LAFAYETTE JAMERSON'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TWENTY DAYS. CROSS-COMPLAINTS HAVE NOT ALLEGED RELIANCE ON THE ACTS OR OMMISSIONS OF CROSS-DEFENDANT. OVERRULED AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PARAGRAPHS 3, 28 THROUGH 43 AND 46 ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS. THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION MAY BE PLEAD IN THE ALTERNATIVE BASED PARAGRAPHS 28 THROUGH 43. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND VS. OFFICE OF ADMINISTRATIVE HEARINGS OF THE STATE OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate ; Request For Judicial Notice; Joint Administrative Record;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JULY 31, 2007, LINE 13. PETITIONER RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY EMPOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate: The writ is denied. The parties agree that the applicable provisions are set forth in the November 7, 2000 Charter amendment, section A.8-587-9. The reasonable interpretation of that section and the Plan as a whole is that the disqualification language is applicable only to service retirement. The fact that the disqualification language is not included in the incapacity section and/or is not set forth in a stand alone section supports this interpretation. In the Court's view, the City's reliance on the 1966 voter initiative supports, rather than undermines, the interpretation because the subsequent amendments do not include (for whatever reason) the blanket and unequivocal disqualification for all forms of retirement. Although the Retirement Board's position is understandable and consistent with the 1966 voter initiative, it appears that this is the first case where a person has had occasion to deny a claim for disability or vesting benefits where the applicant has been convicted of moral turpitude. As a consequence, the Board's decision is not entitled to persuasive weight. Respondent's other arguments for affirmance of the decision by the Administrative Law Judge are unpersuasive. =(302/PJM-DS-X)
